---
title: "CB3 Equity Report — Language Access Map"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import yaml
from shapely import wkt

PROJECT_DIR = Path.cwd()
while not (PROJECT_DIR / "src").exists() and PROJECT_DIR != PROJECT_DIR.parent:
    PROJECT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR.parent

if str(PROJECT_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_DIR))

from src.map_utils import (
    build_grouped_bubble_map,
    find_project_dir,
    format_metric_value,
)

PROJECT_DIR = find_project_dir()
CLEAN_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "language.csv"
METADATA_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "docs" / "metric_metadata.yml"
OUTPUT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "maps" / "language"
OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

OUTPUT_PATH = OUTPUT_DIR / "language_access_lep_households_by_group.html"

EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS = {"senior_poverty_rate"}

# These are the C16002 household language groups prepared by scripts/language/build.py.
# They become separate bubble layers on one shared map.
LANGUAGE_GROUP_METRICS = [
    "spanish_limited_english_household_share",
    "other_indo_european_limited_english_household_share",
    "asian_pacific_limited_english_household_share",
    "other_languages_limited_english_household_share",
]

# Total LEP household share stays available as tooltip context.
TOTAL_LANGUAGE_CONTEXT_METRICS = [
    "lep_household_share",
    "lep_households",
    "households_language_universe",
]
```

```{python}
def to_map_spec(metric, meta, include_filename=False):
    """Convert metric_metadata.yml entries into the spec expected by map_utils."""
    spec = {
        "dimension": meta.get("short_label", metric.replace("_", " ").title()),
        "label": meta.get("label", meta.get("short_label", metric.replace("_", " ").title())),
        "unit": meta.get("unit", ""),
    }

    if "palette" in meta:
        spec["palette"] = meta["palette"]

    if include_filename:
        spec["filename"] = meta.get("filename", f"{metric}.html")

    for optional_key in ("positive_values_only", "zero_color", "zero_label"):
        if optional_key in meta:
            spec[optional_key] = meta[optional_key]

    return spec


with open(METADATA_PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
    all_metrics = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}

# Build specs for the specific language group metrics only.
language_group_specs = {}
for metric in LANGUAGE_GROUP_METRICS:
    meta = all_metrics.get(metric)
    if isinstance(meta, dict):
        language_group_specs[metric] = to_map_spec(metric, meta, include_filename=True)
    else:
        # Fallback so the map can still run if a context metric is present in
        # language.csv but not yet documented in metadata.
        language_group_specs[metric] = {
            "dimension": metric.replace("_", " ").replace("Limited English Household Share", "LEP household share").title(),
            "label": metric.replace("_", " ").title(),
            "unit": "%",
            "filename": OUTPUT_PATH.name,
        }

demographic_specs = {
    metric: to_map_spec(metric, meta)
    for metric, meta in all_metrics.items()
    if isinstance(meta, dict)
    and meta.get("domain") == "demographics"
    and metric not in EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS
}

if not demographic_specs:
    raise ValueError("No demographic metrics found in metric_metadata.yml.")

print("Language group bubble metrics:")
for metric in language_group_specs:
    print(f"  {metric}")

print("\nDemographic backdrop metrics:")
for metric in demographic_specs:
    print(f"  {metric}")
```

## Load prepared clean map file

```{python}
if not CLEAN_PATH.exists():
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        f"Could not find {CLEAN_PATH}. Run scripts/language/build.py first."
    )

clean = pd.read_csv(CLEAN_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"}, low_memory=False)

required_columns = {
    "GEOID",
    "tract_label",
    "nta_name",
    "tract_centroid_latitude",
    "tract_centroid_longitude",
    "geometry_wkt",
}
missing_required = sorted(required_columns - set(clean.columns))
if missing_required:
    raise ValueError(
        "language.csv is not map-ready. Missing columns: "
        + ", ".join(missing_required)
        + ". Re-run the refactored scripts/language/build.py."
    )

clean["GEOID"] = clean["GEOID"].astype(str).str.extract(r"(\d+)")[0].str.zfill(11)
clean["geometry"] = clean["geometry_wkt"].map(wkt.loads)

tracts = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    clean.drop(columns=["geometry_wkt"]),
    geometry="geometry",
    crs="EPSG:4326",
)

numeric_columns = set(LANGUAGE_GROUP_METRICS) | set(TOTAL_LANGUAGE_CONTEXT_METRICS) | set(demographic_specs)
for column in numeric_columns:
    if column in tracts.columns:
        tracts[column] = pd.to_numeric(tracts[column], errors="coerce")

available_language_group_specs = {
    metric: spec
    for metric, spec in language_group_specs.items()
    if metric in tracts.columns and tracts[metric].notna().any()
}

available_demographic_specs = {
    metric: spec
    for metric, spec in demographic_specs.items()
    if metric in tracts.columns and tracts[metric].notna().any()
}

assert len(tracts) == 31, f"Expected 31 CB3 tracts, got {len(tracts)}"
assert tracts["GEOID"].is_unique

if not available_language_group_specs:
    raise ValueError(
        "No LEP household language-group metrics are available in data/clean/language.csv."
    )

if not available_demographic_specs:
    raise ValueError(
        "No demographic backdrop columns are available in data/clean/language.csv. "
        "Make sure build.py merged a baseline file or that language.csv already contains demographics."
    )

print("Available language group bubble layers:")
for metric, spec in available_language_group_specs.items():
    print(f"  {metric}: {tracts[metric].notna().sum()} tracts")

print("\nAvailable demographic backdrops:")
for metric in available_demographic_specs:
    print(f"  {metric}: {tracts[metric].notna().sum()} tracts")
```

## Add total LEP household context to tooltip

```{python}
def add_display_column(source_column, label, unit):
    display_column = f"{source_column}_display"
    tracts[display_column] = tracts[source_column].map(
        lambda value: format_metric_value(value, unit)
    )
    return display_column


tooltip_fields = ["tract_label", "nta_name"]
tooltip_aliases = ["Census tract", "NTA"]

context_specs = {
    "lep_household_share": ("Total LEP household share", "%"),
    "lep_households": ("Total LEP households", ""),
    "households_language_universe": ("Household language universe", ""),
}

for source_column, (label, unit) in context_specs.items():
    if source_column in tracts.columns and tracts[source_column].notna().any():
        tooltip_fields.append(add_display_column(source_column, label, unit))
        tooltip_aliases.append(label)
```

## Render grouped Language Access map

```{python}
language_map = build_grouped_bubble_map(
    tracts=tracts,
    metric_specs=available_language_group_specs,
    demographic_specs=available_demographic_specs,
    metrics=list(available_language_group_specs),
    title="CB3 Language Access: LEP households by language group",
    subtitle=(
        "Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. "
        "Bubbles: selectable C16002 limited-English household language groups. "
        "Tooltip includes total LEP household context where available."
    ),
    output_path=OUTPUT_PATH,
    tooltip_fields=tooltip_fields,
    tooltip_aliases=tooltip_aliases,
)

print(f"Saved grouped Language Access map: {OUTPUT_PATH.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```

## Preview map

```{python}
language_map
```

## Validation summary

```{python}
summary = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "metric": metric,
            "label": available_language_group_specs[metric]["label"],
            "tracts_with_data": int(tracts[metric].notna().sum()),
            "output_html": OUTPUT_PATH.name,
        }
        for metric in available_language_group_specs
    ]
)

display(summary)

assert OUTPUT_PATH.exists(), OUTPUT_PATH
assert OUTPUT_PATH.stat().st_size > 10_000, OUTPUT_PATH

print("Output file:")
print(f"  {OUTPUT_PATH.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```
